title: Deal velocity from marketing sources accelerated this week
anchor: Current week marketing-sourced wins closed at 38 days versus the prior 11-week average of 50.7 days.
connect: Nine wins this week included seven mid-market deals.
body: Current week marketing-sourced wins closed at 38 days versus the prior 11-week average of 50.7 days. Nine wins this week included seven mid-market deals.
trace: Significant velocity improvement in marketing-attributed deals, concentrated in mid-market segment.

title: Enterprise win rate climbed eight points in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise deals closed at 30.77% win rate versus the trailing four-quarter average of 22.22%.
connect: Average deal size increased from $142K to $187K over the same comparison.
body: Q2 enterprise deals closed at 30.77% win rate versus the trailing four-quarter average of 22.22%. Average deal size increased from $142K to $187K over the same comparison.
trace: Both win rate and deal size moving upward in enterprise segment this quarter.

title: Event pipeline velocity compressed by 24 days in Q1
anchor: SaaS Connect and peer events sourced 23 wins at 45.3 days average cycle versus Q4's 69.9 days.
connect: SaaS Connect contributed 14 of the 23 event-sourced wins.
body: SaaS Connect and peer events sourced 23 wins at 45.3 days average cycle versus Q4's 69.9 days. SaaS Connect contributed 14 of the 23 event-sourced wins.
trace: Event pipeline moving faster through the funnel, with SaaS Connect as the primary driver.

title: Mid-market SQL volume jumped 50% in April
anchor: April mid-market SQLs sit at 147 month-to-date versus the Q1 monthly average of 98.
connect: The Q1 pattern ran steady at 95, 98, and 101 SQLs per month.
body: April mid-market SQLs sit at 147 month-to-date versus the Q1 monthly average of 98. The Q1 pattern ran steady at 95, 98, and 101 SQLs per month.
trace: Significant uptick in mid-market SQL generation, breaking out of the Q1 steady-state pattern.

title: ABM acceptance rate leads non-ABM by 20 points
anchor: April ABM MQLs convert to SDR-accepted at 61.4% versus 41.6% for non-ABM MQLs.
connect: ABM volume totaled 70 MQLs this month versus 77 non-ABM MQLs.
body: April ABM MQLs convert to SDR-accepted at 61.4% versus 41.6% for non-ABM MQLs. ABM volume totaled 70 MQLs this month versus 77 non-ABM MQLs.
trace: ABM MQLs showing superior qualification rates despite similar volume levels.

title: Paid social pipeline outpaced paid search in Q2
anchor: Paid social generated $620K pipeline in Q2 versus paid search at $380K.
connect: Q1 patterns ran opposite with paid search at $720K and paid social at $410K.
body: Paid social generated $620K pipeline in Q2 versus paid search at $380K. Q1 patterns ran opposite with paid search at $720K and paid social at $410K.
trace: Channel productivity flip between quarters, with paid social taking the lead in Q2.

title: Digital ads budget carries $84K headroom in Q2
anchor: Actual digital spend sits at $256K through April 23 versus the $340K Q2 plan.
connect: The unspent budget reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
body: Actual digital spend sits at $256K through April 23 versus the $340K Q2 plan. The unspent budget reallocated to SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events.
trace: Under-paced digital spend being redirected to event marketing investments.

title: Branded search volume doubled over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921 over six consecutive weeks with no week-over-week declines.
connect: The cumulative growth rate reached 92.1% through April 19.
body: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921 over six consecutive weeks with no week-over-week declines. The cumulative growth rate reached 92.1% through April 19.
trace: Consistent upward trajectory in branded search without any weekly reversals.

title: Atlas mentions lead Beacon Systems by 52% in April
anchor: Atlas SaaS captured 1840 brand mentions versus Beacon Systems at 1210 through April 24.
connect: Beacon Systems sits as the top competitor in the named competitive set.
body: Atlas SaaS captured 1840 brand mentions versus Beacon Systems at 1210 through April 24. Beacon Systems sits as the top competitor in the named competitive set.
trace: Atlas maintaining mention share leadership against primary competitor Beacon Systems.

title: Analyst attention increased 50% in the last two weeks
anchor: Analyst mentions hit nine in the last 14 days versus six in the prior 30 days.
connect: Forrester and G2 each contributed three mentions with Gartner adding one.
body: Analyst mentions hit nine in the last 14 days versus six in the prior 30 days. Forrester and G2 each contributed three mentions with Gartner adding one.
trace: Accelerating analyst coverage with balanced distribution across top-tier firms.

title: Direct traffic crossed organic search for the first time
anchor: April traffic runs at 42.4% direct versus 36.4% organic search on a cumulative basis.
connect: The crossover point occurred on April 15.
body: April traffic runs at 42.4% direct versus 36.4% organic search on a cumulative basis. The crossover point occurred on April 15.
trace: Brand-driven traffic now exceeding search-driven traffic for the first time.

title: Atlas Insights reference opt-in rate sits at 76%
anchor: Atlas Insights customers opt into reference programs at 75.6% compared to Atlas Workflow at 71.1%.
connect: Atlas Connect reference participation lags at 27.5%.
body: Atlas Insights customers opt into reference programs at 75.6% compared to Atlas Workflow at 71.1%. Atlas Connect reference participation lags at 27.5%.
trace: Strong reference pool availability in core products, with Connect showing lower engagement.

title: Target accounts with high intent doubled this week
anchor: Three target accounts logged five or more high-intent events in the current week.
connect: The activity splits between March ABM Add list and Named Accounts with two accounts from Named Accounts.
body: Three target accounts logged five or more high-intent events in the current week. The activity splits between March ABM Add list and Named Accounts with two accounts from Named Accounts.
trace: Increased engagement velocity among target accounts, concentrated in Named Accounts list.

title: Atlas Assist launch campaign locks this Friday
anchor: Atlas Assist ships May 8 with the associated campaign marked ready.
body: Atlas Assist ships May 8 with the associated campaign marked ready. Campaign calendar locks April 24.
trace: Product launch coordination on schedule with marketing campaign infrastructure complete.

title: SDR inbound capacity exceeds demand by 48%
anchor: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 versus 142 inbound leads.
connect: The trailing four-week pattern averaged 214 capacity versus 193 lead volume.
body: Current week SDR capacity sits at 210 versus 142 inbound leads. The trailing four-week pattern averaged 214 capacity versus 193 lead volume.
trace: SDR team currently operating with significant capacity headroom versus inbound flow.

title: Fintech segment concentration shows up in recent mid-market wins
anchor: Last 60 days of mid-market closed-wins included eight fintech companies out of 12 total wins.
connect: All 12 wins ran Snowflake plus dbt in their data stack.
body: Last 60 days of mid-market closed-wins included eight fintech companies out of 12 total wins. All 12 wins ran Snowflake plus dbt in their data stack.
trace: Strong vertical and tech stack patterns emerging in recent mid-market success.

title: Q2 event budget reallocated from digital underspend
anchor: SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events received $84K from the digital ads underspend.
connect: Digital ads actual spend sits at $256K versus the $340K Q2 plan.
body: SaaS Connect and Signal Summit events received $84K from the digital ads underspend. Digital ads actual spend sits at $256K versus the $340K Q2 plan.
trace: Budget flexibility allowing for event investment using digital ads savings.

title: Marketing-sourced velocity improved across 69 wins this quarter
anchor: Prior 11 weeks of marketing-sourced deals closed at 50.7 days mean cycle time.
connect: Current week velocity sits at 38 days across nine marketing-sourced wins.
body: Prior 11 weeks of marketing-sourced deals closed at 50.7 days mean cycle time. Current week velocity sits at 38 days across nine marketing-sourced wins.
trace: Consistent pipeline from marketing sources with recent acceleration in deal velocity.
